description: You are a hospitalist reviewing a chest CT for a patient admitted with hypertension and exertional dyspnea. Assess for cardiomegaly by calculating the cardiothoracic ratio (CTR) using the CT series at /home/ga/DICOM/studies/chest_ct/. On the axial slice showing the maximum transverse cardiac diameter, measure both the cardiac width and the widest inner thoracic diameter. Export the annotated slice to /home/ga/DICOM/exports/cardiac_analysis.png and save a report containing the cardiac width (mm), thoracic width (mm), and calculated CTR (decimal) to /home/ga/DICOM/exports/cardiac_report.txt. A CTR above 0.50 suggests cardiomegaly.
